cubes.title: Basic error handling with try/except and await
rules: Wrap await calls in try/except blocks to catch exceptions from coroutines.
Exceptions are raised when the awaited coroutine completes or fails.
cubes.rationale: Asynchronous code uses the same try/except syntax as synchronous code, but exceptions are raised at the point of await, allowing standard error handling within coroutines.

cubes.title: Handling errors in asyncio.gather (default behavior)
rules: When using asyncio.gather without return_exceptions=True, an exception in any task propagates immediately and cancels other pending tasks.
Wrap gather in try/except to catch the first exception.
cubes.rationale: By default, asyncio.gather raises the first exception encountered, which cancels all other tasks. This is useful for fail-fast scenarios but may lose results from other tasks.

cubes.title: Handling errors in asyncio.gather with return_exceptions=True
rules: Set return_exceptions=True in asyncio.gather to return exceptions as results instead of raising them.
Then iterate over results to check for exceptions using isinstance(result, Exception).
cubes.rationale: Using return_exceptions=True allows all tasks to complete and returns exceptions as part of the result list, enabling handling of multiple failures without cancelling other tasks.

cubes.title: Three-layer file structure for knowledge base projects
rules: src/ contains application code including main.py, routers, middleware, models
knowledge_library/ contains public knowledge with cubes (basic and experience) and anketa
runtime/ contains private context with personas, sessions, and storage
cubes.rationale: This separation keeps code separate from public knowledge and private runtime data, improving modularity, security, and maintainability.

cubes.title: Manual SSL Certificate Injection in nginx-proxy with acme-companion
rules: When automatic certificate generation via acme-companion fails
When you need to manually inject certificates for a specific domain
When you have certificates from an external CA
cubes.rationale: This procedure provides a fallback to manually copy certificate files (fullchain and private key) into nginx-proxy's expected location (/etc/nginx/certs/) with the correct naming (.crt and .key), and ensures ssl_reject_handshake is off to prevent SSL errors. It is useful when automatic acquisition fails or external certificates need to be used.

cubes.title: Organize project files and create safe backup before refactoring
rules: Create a dated backup of the current file before making any significant change using a timestamp to ensure uniqueness and easy rollback: cp file.py file.py.backup-$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M)
Keep only the latest working version in the active directory; after refactoring and testing, remove older backups from the active directory.
Move old versions to a dedicated archive/ folder at the project root to keep the active directory clean: mkdir -p archive; mv file.py.backup-* archive/
Organize archived files by type or module inside archive/ to make retrieval easier.
cubes.rationale: Before refactoring, proper organization and backup are critical to avoid losing work and to enable easy rollback.
